Mechanical Ventilator Simulator

Real-time respiratory mechanics simulation

PS

VT

Flow

Target VT

%

Pmax

RR

I-Time

%

FiO₂

%

Measurements

PIP

Plateau

RR

VTE

ml/kg PBW

MV

P0.1

Adjust Ventilator

Invalid I:E ratio - no expiratory time. Reduce rate, I-time, or pause.

Patient & Scenarios

Patient Characteristics

Presets:

Patient Demographics (optional):

Time Constant (τ): seconds

95% equilibration: s

Elastic Recoil

Higher = sharper peak exp flow spike and pressure recoil during Pmus relaxation.

Additional volume released when PEEP decreases from ≥15

(useful for teaching the recruitability maneuver to calculate R/I ratio)

Normal airways (no closure) Simulates airway closure

(useful for teaching how to check for Airway Closure)

Stress Index Abnormality

SI 0.9-1.1: Normal (linear rise)

Titrating PEEP will affect the stress index

Upper Inflection Point

Moderate UIP: Compliance reduction at higher pressures

Alveolar pressure above this threshold reduces effective compliance

Reverse Triggering

Patient effort entrained to every Nth mandatory breath

Reverse triggering: Machine breath triggers patient effort (Pmus occurs after breath onset). Breath-stacked breaths are ignored.

Spontaneous Breathing

Peak inspiratory effort

Responsiveness: %
Low (e.g. anxious / agitated) High (relaxes quickly)
Patient unloads as ventilator does more work; effort grows when support drops. Amplitude, rise, hold, and decay all scale together. Baseline anchors at current support level when toggled on or when Pmus is edited.

Pmus rises over Trise, holds at peak (Thold), then decays (PIIA) over Tdecay. Decay continues into early expiration.

Cycle shifted forward (+s) Cycle shifted backward (s) Synchronized

(display upward)

Cardiac Oscillations

Cardiogenic oscillations can auto-trigger with sensitive flow triggers

Cardiac oscillations: Heart beating against lungs creates small flow/pressure variations (~4 L/min, ~1 cmH₂O) that can trigger breaths with sensitive flow trigger settings.

Natural Variability

Standard deviation as % of mean (5-20%)

Breath-to-breath variability: Adds Gaussian-distributed noise to compliance, resistance, spontaneous breathing rate, and patient effort to simulate real clinical conditions.

Expiratory Flow Limitation

Critical Closing Pressure:
Flow Conductance (k):
PEEP Stenting Effect:

Pressure below which airways collapse

Higher = less flow limitation

How effectively PEEP prevents airway collapse

Obstructive physiology: Airways collapse during expiration, creating flow limitation and auto-PEEP. Increase external PEEP to stent airways open and reduce air trapping.

Pressure (cmH₂O) @ s: cmH₂O, Pmus: cmH₂O

Tag:
▲ Triggered
▲ Triggered

Flow (L/min) @ s: L/min

Volume (mL) @ s: mL

This simulator was created by Thomas Piraino RRT, FCSRT, FAARC and is for education purposes only.

Asynchrony examples:

Note: Reverse triggering (Early Trigger) uses the 'Entrainment' settings under Patient Characteristics in the Settings panel (only available in control modes).
